children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, store in patients data store, and return success or error

semantic_outputs.name: result
semantic_outputs.description: JSON with success status, patient ID, and message

postconditions: patient record created in patients data store

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and appointment details from input, verify patient exists, check slot availability, store appointment in appointments data store, and return confirmation

semantic_inputs.name: input
semantic_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and appointment details

semantic_outputs.name: result
semantic_outputs.description: JSON with success status, appointment ID, and message

postconditions: appointment record created in appointments data store

children.name: GetPatientRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID from input, verify patient exists, read records from patients data store (append-only), and return records

semantic_outputs.name: result
semantic_outputs.description: JSON with success status, records data, and message

children.name: UpdatePatientProfile
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and updated fields from input, verify patient exists, update profile in patients data store, and return success

semantic_inputs.name: input
semantic_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and updated profile fields

semantic_outputs.name: result
semantic_outputs.description: JSON with success status and message

postconditions: patient profile updated in patients data store

decomposition_rationale: The parent PatientPortal handles four distinct operations: registration, appointment booking, record retrieval, and profile update. Each operation is independent and can be implemented as a separate child function. The parent routes the input command to the appropriate child based on the command field, ensuring no sibling calls.
